levels.title: Quran basics
levels.blurb: Well-known verses on Allah, the Prophet, and core beliefs. Open the source and the answer is right there.

questions.q: Al-Fatiha opens with praise to Allah. Verse 2 calls Him 'Lord of the ___'
Ayat al-Kursi says Allah is never overtaken by 'drowsiness or ___'
Q 2:256 says 'there shall be no ___ in [acceptance of] the religion'
Q 33:40 gives Muhammad a unique title among all the prophets. What is it?
The very first word of the first revelation to Muhammad was '___  in the name of your Lord who created'
Q 112:3 says Allah 'neither begets nor ___'
Q 4:3 sets a maximum on the number of wives a Muslim man may have. What is it?
Q 18:86 says Dhul-Qarnayn followed the sun until he found it setting in a spring of dark mud — TRUE or FALSE
Q 4:34 opens with a statement about authority in the household. What does it say men are over women?
questions.b: In charge, by right of what Allah has given one over the other
questions.c: Brothers and protectors in the faith

questions.q: Q 67:5 says the stars of the nearest heaven were also made 'missiles against the ___'
Q 2:106 explains abrogation. When Allah causes a verse to be forgotten or replaces it, what does He bring instead?
questions.a: A clarification delivered through the Prophet
questions.b: One better than it or similar to it
questions.c: Silence, as a test for believers

questions.q: Q 2:23 challenges doubters of the Quran. What does it dare them to produce?

levels.title: Aisha & the household
levels.blurb: The Prophet's marriages, household rules, and the Quran's instructions to his wives. Every answer is in the linked chapter.

questions.q: Bukhari records the age at which Muhammad married Aisha. What age does it give?
Bukhari records the age at which Aisha's marriage to the Prophet was consummated. What age does it give?
Q 33:37 describes how Zaynab came to be married to the Prophet after her husband Zayd divorced her. Who does the verse say arranged the marriage?
questions.b: Allah — the verse says 'We married her to you'
questions.c: The Prophet himself, after Zayd gave his blessing

questions.q: Q 33:53 places a permanent prohibition on all believers regarding the Prophet's wives. What is it?
questions.a: Entering the Prophet's home without an invitation
questions.b: Marrying any of his wives after him, ever
questions.c: Speaking to his wives without a screen between

questions.q: 'The Prophet is more worthy of the believers than their own ___'
Q 33:50 grants the Prophet the right to marry women who offer themselves to him without a dowry, and explicitly states this allowance is only for him, not other believers — TRUE or FALSE
Q 66:1 opens with Allah rebuking the Prophet. What had the Prophet done to prompt the rebuke?
questions.a: Neglected his prayer duties for several days
questions.b: Prohibited something Allah had made lawful for him
questions.c: Shown clear favouritism to one wife over the others

questions.q: Aisha describes in Bukhari what she was playing with when the Prophet would come and join her. What was she playing with?
Q 24:11 opens the passage defending Aisha's honour with the words: 'Indeed, those who came with the ___'
Q 33:28 describes what the Prophet must give to any wife who chooses this world over him. What does she receive?
questions.a: Half her dowry and the right to remarry
questions.b: A gracious release from the marriage
questions.c: Her freedom but no financial support

questions.q: Q 33:4 states that Allah has NOT made the Prophet's adopted son Zayd his true son — TRUE or FALSE
Q 33:56 commands believers to do something specific regarding the Prophet. What does it command?
questions.a: Defend him with their lives and wealth
questions.b: Ask Allah to confer blessing on him and salute him with respect
questions.c: Follow his example in all private and public matters

levels.title: Daily-life weirdness
levels.blurb: Strange prescriptions on medicine, hygiene, prayer, and daily behaviour — all straight from Bukhari and Muslim.

questions.q: Bukhari says if a fly falls in your drink you should dip the whole fly in before removing it. What reason does the hadith give?
questions.a: One wing carries disease and the other carries the cure
questions.b: The fly neutralises any impurity when fully submerged
questions.c: Wasting food is forbidden and the drink remains pure regardless

questions.q: Bukhari records a prescription the Prophet gave to ill people involving camels. What was it?
questions.b: Camel urine mixed with camel milk
questions.c: Rendered camel fat applied to the skin

questions.q: Bukhari records that Satan 'spends the night in the ___ part of your nose' if you do not rinse at dawn
Muslim records that a black dog, a donkey, or a woman passing in front of a praying man cuts off his prayer — TRUE or FALSE
Bukhari says the rooster crows at night because it has seen something. What has it seen?
Bukhari records that the Prophet was bewitched. What was the specific effect of the bewitchment on him?
questions.a: He forgot entire passages of the Quran for a period
questions.b: He imagined he had done things he had not actually done
questions.c: He was unable to speak for several days

questions.q: According to Bukhari, the Prophet had the physical strength of ___ men, which allowed him to visit all his wives in one night
Bukhari records a rule about the number of stones used when cleansing after the toilet. What is the rule?
questions.a: There must be at least five stones

questions.q: Muslim records the Prophet's instruction on drinking water. In what position should a person drink?
Bukhari records that the Prophet's recommended sleeping position is on the right side — TRUE or FALSE
Bukhari records the Prophet's instruction on dyeing grey hair. Whose appearance must the dyeing not imitate?
Muslim records what the Prophet said about eating the monitor lizard (dabb). What was his position?
questions.a: It is forbidden to eat under all circumstances
questions.b: It must be slaughtered like other animals to be lawful
questions.c: He neither ate it himself nor prohibited others from eating it

levels.title: Cosmology & nature
levels.blurb: The Quran's account of creation, the heavens, the earth, the human body, and the sun.

questions.q: Q 21:30 makes a claim about the original state of the heavens and earth. What does it say they were before Allah separated them?
How many heavens does Q 65:12 say Allah created?
Q 78:7 uses a single word to describe what mountains do for the earth. What is it?
Q 86:6-7 describes where human seed originates in the male body. Where does the verse place its origin?
questions.b: Between the backbone and the ribs
questions.c: From the loins near the heart

questions.q: A Bukhari hadith says the sun prostrates under Allah's Throne each evening and asks permission before it is allowed to rise again — TRUE or FALSE
Q 22:5 describes embryonic development. Which sequence of stages does the verse actually list?
questions.a: A seed, then a clot, then a fully formed child
questions.b: Dust, then a clinging clot, then bones, then flesh
questions.c: A drop of fluid, a clinging clot, a clump of flesh formed and unformed, then bones clothed with flesh

questions.q: Q 71:16 describes what the sun is. What term does the verse use for it?
Q 79:30 — 'And after that He ___ the earth'
Q 25:54 names a specific substance from which man was created. What is it?
Q 38:71 records Allah telling the angels what He is about to create man from. What is the substance?
questions.b: Nothing — He creates by His word alone

questions.q: Q 23:14 describes what happens after the bones form in the womb. What does the verse say happens to the bones at that stage?
questions.a: The spirit is breathed into them
questions.c: The limbs are extended and completed

questions.q: Q 25:54 says man was created from water. Q 38:71 says from clay. Open both — which answer does Q 96:2 give as a third account of man's origin?

levels.title: Warfare & conquest
levels.blurb: Quranic commands on fighting, the treatment of enemies, and the division of war spoils.

questions.q: Q 9:5, the 'Sword Verse,' commands what be done to polytheists who have not repented, prayed, and given zakah?
questions.a: Expel them from the Arabian Peninsula
questions.c: Give them a final chance to pay the jizyah

questions.q: Q 9:29 commands believers to fight a specific group until they pay the jizyah 'while they are humbled.' Who are they?
questions.a: All polytheists who refuse to convert
questions.b: Those among the People of the Book who do not forbid what Allah and His Messenger have forbidden
questions.c: Only those who have actively attacked Muslim lands

questions.q: Q 8:41 divides war booty, reserving 'one ___ of it for Allah and for the Messenger'
Q 8:12 records what Allah said He would cast into the hearts of the disbelievers. What was it?
Q 47:4 instructs believers on what to do when they meet disbelievers in battle. What does the verse say to do first?
questions.a: Offer terms of peace before any attack
questions.b: Strike their necks, then bind them — and afterwards either ransom or free them
questions.c: Drive them from their lands without bloodshed where possible

questions.q: Q 2:216 says fighting has been 'enjoined upon you while it is hateful to you' — TRUE or FALSE
Q 9:111 describes a transaction between Allah and the believers. What does Allah say He has purchased from them in exchange for Paradise?
Q 8:65 gives a ratio: one steadfast believer can overcome how many disbelievers?
Q 5:33 lists the punishments for those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger. Which of these is the complete list given in the verse?
questions.b: Death, crucifixion, cutting of opposite hands and feet, or exile from the land
questions.c: Flogging, exile, or execution by any means

questions.q: Q 9:14 describes the role of Muslim fighters in relation to Allah's punishment. How does the verse frame it?
questions.a: Believers witness Allah's judgment on the enemy
questions.b: Allah punishes the disbelievers by the hands of the believers
questions.c: Believers earn a double reward for their sacrifice in battle

questions.q: Open both sources. Q 3:151 and Q 8:12 both contain Allah's promise to cast terror into the hearts of disbelievers — TRUE or FALSE
Q 4:89 addresses hypocrites who refuse to emigrate and fight alongside believers. What does the verse say to do with them?
questions.a: Leave them alone as long as they do not attack Muslims
questions.b: Take them and kill them wherever found
questions.c: Banish them from the Muslim community

levels.title: Hudud & punishment
levels.blurb: Precise Quranic and hadith punishments — exact numbers, conditions, and scope. Check the verse.

questions.q: Q 24:2 prescribes a punishment for the unmarried fornicator and gives a condition for applying it. What is the punishment, and what must not influence its application?
questions.a: Forty lashes in private — compassion must not interfere
questions.b: One hundred lashes before believers — pity must not interfere
questions.c: Eighty lashes by the ruler — family must not be present

questions.q: Q 5:38 prescribes a specific punishment for theft. What is it?
questions.a: Imprisonment until the stolen property is returned
questions.b: Public flogging and repayment of double the value

questions.q: Bukhari records the Prophet's ruling on apostasy — leaving Islam. What does he command be done to the apostate?
questions.a: Debate them and give them three days to repent
questions.b: Exile them permanently from Muslim territory

questions.q: Q 24:4 — the false accuser who cannot produce four witnesses 'shall be flogged with ___ lashes'
Q 4:56 describes what happens to disbelievers' skin in Hell. What happens, and why?
questions.a: It is burned away to expose the soul, which cannot feel pain
questions.b: It is replaced with new skin so they keep tasting the punishment
questions.c: It turns black permanently as a mark of their sin

questions.q: Q 4:15 describes the punishment for women proven guilty of lewdness before a definitive ruling was established. What was it?
questions.b: House confinement until death or until Allah ordains another way

questions.q: Q 5:45 quotes the law of equal retaliation. Complete the sequence: 'a life for a life, an eye for an eye, and a nose for a ___'
Q 5:33 lists punishments for those who wage war on Allah. Besides death, what other punishments does the verse name?
questions.b: Crucifixion, cutting of opposite hands and feet, and exile
questions.c: Flogging, exile, and confiscation of property

questions.q: Q 24:2 states that the flogging punishment must be witnessed by a group of believers — TRUE or FALSE
Q 24:2 prescribes 100 lashes for the unmarried fornicator. Open both sources — what does Bukhari record as the punishment for a married person who commits adultery?
questions.c: One hundred lashes plus a year of exile

questions.q: Q 5:38 describes the cutting of the hand as 'an exemplary punishment from Allah' — TRUE or FALSE
Q 33:57 addresses those who harm or annoy Allah and His Messenger. What does the verse say Allah has done to them?
questions.a: Warned them of a doubled punishment if they do not repent
questions.b: Cursed them in this world and the hereafter and prepared for them a humiliating punishment
questions.c: Commanded that they be expelled from the community of believers

levels.title: Privileges of the Prophet
levels.blurb: Unique divine allowances granted exclusively to Muhammad — stated openly in his own scripture.

questions.q: Q 33:50 lists several marriage categories uniquely lawful for the Prophet. Which of these is actually in the verse?
questions.a: The right to marry up to nine wives simultaneously
questions.b: Women who offer themselves to him, female captives from war, and his cousins — without the dowry requirements that bind other believers

questions.q: Q 33:51 gives the Prophet special authority over his wives' rotation. What does it permit him to do?
questions.a: Keep all wives on an equal rotation at all times
questions.b: Put aside whom he will or take to himself whom he will
questions.c: Dismiss a wife permanently if she causes distress

questions.q: Q 33:50 explicitly states that the special marriage allowances granted to the Prophet are only for him, not for other believers — TRUE or FALSE
Q 33:21 gives the Prophet a title that frames him as the standard for Muslim behaviour. What does it call him?
questions.a: The greatest man who ever lived
questions.b: A mercy to all the worlds

questions.q: Q 4:65 sets a condition for having true faith. What must believers do specifically regarding the Prophet?
questions.a: Pray for him five times daily and support his household financially
questions.b: Make him judge in all their disputes and find no discomfort in his rulings
questions.c: Obey him exactly as they obey Allah without any questioning

questions.q: Q 33:4 says Allah has NOT made Zayd the Prophet's true son. Q 33:37 then says Allah married Zayd's ex-wife Zaynab to the Prophet. Open both — how do these two verses work together in the Quran's logic?
questions.a: They show the Prophet was simply exempt from normal family law
questions.b: By declaring Zayd not a true son, the verse removes the normal prohibition on marrying a son's ex-wife, making the Zaynab marriage lawful
questions.c: They demonstrate that Zayd voluntarily stepped aside and requested the marriage

questions.q: Q 8:41 divides war spoils. What share does the verse say belongs to Allah and the Prophet?
Bukhari records that the Prophet had the physical strength of thirty men — TRUE or FALSE
Q 33:53 says something is 'not lawful' regarding the Prophet. What is it?
questions.a: Questioning any of his decisions or rulings
questions.c: Entering his presence without an explicit invitation

questions.q: Q 66:1 opens with Allah questioning the Prophet about something he had done. What had he done?
questions.a: Delayed prayer on multiple occasions without excuse
questions.b: Prohibited something Allah had made lawful for him
questions.c: Favoured one wife so consistently that others complained

questions.q: Q 33:6 states that the Prophet holds a unique rank over believers. What exactly does the verse say?
questions.a: He must be obeyed before parents and leaders
questions.b: He is more worthy of the believers than their own selves, and his wives are their mothers
questions.c: He is owed the same obedience that is owed to Allah

questions.q: Q 53:3-4 makes a claim about the nature of the Prophet's speech. What does it say?
questions.a: His speech is the wisest of all men, guided by divine insight
questions.b: He does not speak from his own inclination — it is nothing but a revelation revealed to him
questions.c: His judgments carry the combined weight of all the angels

levels.title: Logical & textual contradictions
levels.blurb: Tensions between Quranic verses, and between the Quran and the hadiths. Open both sources and look carefully.

questions.q: Q 2:256 says 'there is no compulsion in religion.' Q 9:5 commands killing polytheists who do not repent and pray. Open both — how do these two verses conflict?
questions.a: They don't conflict — Q 2:256 applies only to People of the Book, not polytheists
questions.b: Q 2:256 forbids forced conversion in principle, but Q 9:5 mandates killing those who refuse to convert — the ultimate form of compulsion
questions.c: Q 9:5 was revealed before Q 2:256 and was fully abrogated by it

questions.q: Q 4:82 says if the Quran were from other than Allah there would be 'much contradiction' in it. What does this verse use as proof of divine authorship?
questions.a: The Quran was reviewed by a council of scholars and found consistent
questions.b: The claimed absence of contradiction is itself the proof
questions.c: The Quran's grammatical consistency proves a single author

questions.q: Q 7:54 says creation took six days. Open Q 41:9-12 and add up the days listed: two for the earth, four for its provisions, two for the seven heavens. What is the total?
Q 4:157 addresses the Christian claim that Jesus was crucified. What does the verse say actually happened?
questions.a: Jesus was crucified but did not die — he was taken up while still alive
questions.b: Jesus was not killed or crucified — it only appeared so because another was made to resemble him
questions.c: Jesus ascended to heaven moments before the crucifixion could occur

questions.q: Q 25:54 says man was created from water. Q 38:71 says clay. Open Q 96:2 — what third substance does it give as the origin of man?
Q 4:171 calls Jesus 'a word from Allah' and 'a spirit from Him,' then immediately gives a prohibition. What does it forbid saying?

Bukhari records that the Prophet was successfully bewitched and began imagining things he had not done. This sits in tension with the doctrine that prophets are divinely protected from the effects of magic — TRUE or FALSE
Q 5:3 says 'This day I have perfected your religion for you.' Q 2:106 says Allah abrogates verses and replaces them with better ones. Open both — why do these create a tension?
questions.a: A perfected religion should not require internal cancellations and replacements of its own scripture
questions.b: Q 5:3 was revealed first and Q 2:106 was meant to explain it
questions.c: Abrogation only applies to earlier scriptures like the Torah, not the Quran itself

questions.q: Q 4:43 says 'do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated.' Open Q 5:90 — what does that later verse declare about intoxicants?
questions.a: Q 5:90 permits them in moderation, contradicting Q 4:43's partial restriction
questions.b: Q 5:90 calls them an abomination and the work of Satan, and commands avoidance
questions.c: Q 5:90 restricts them to specific times but does not ban them outright

questions.q: Q 3:85 says whoever desires a religion other than Islam will never have it accepted. What does the verse say their fate in the hereafter will be?
questions.a: They will receive a lesser reward unless they convert before death
questions.b: It will never be accepted and they will be among the losers
questions.c: They have until the Day of Judgment to reconsider

questions.q: Q 2:62 says Jews, Christians, and Sabeans who believe in Allah and do good will have their reward. Q 3:85 says no religion other than Islam is accepted. Open both — what is the apparent conflict?
questions.a: There is no conflict — Q 2:62 applies only to those who lived before Muhammad
questions.b: Q 2:62 appears to promise salvation to practising Jews and Christians, while Q 3:85 flatly denies that any non-Islamic faith will be accepted
questions.c: Q 3:85 applies only to those who explicitly rejected Islam after being exposed to it

questions.q: Q 3:59 compares the creation of Jesus to the creation of Adam — both created without human fathers — TRUE or FALSE

levels.title: The deep cuts
levels.blurb: Obscure hadiths, the night journey, paradise rewards, and a verse eaten by a goat. All in the source.

questions.q: Tirmidhi records the original condition of the Black Stone and what changed it. What does the hadith say?
questions.a: It was grey and rough — pilgrims' hands polished it dark over centuries
questions.b: It was whiter than milk — the sins of the children of Adam blackened it
questions.c: It was red like a ruby — exposure to the elements darkened it

questions.q: Bukhari's Mi'raj account says daily prayers were originally commanded at a number far above five. What was the original number, and who negotiated them down?
questions.a: Thirty prayers — the Prophet himself asked Allah to reduce them
questions.b: Fifty prayers — Moses told Muhammad to keep going back and asking for reductions
questions.c: Forty prayers — the angels interceded on behalf of humanity

questions.q: During the Mi'raj, Muhammad encountered the prophets in the heavens. In which heaven did he meet Ibrahim (Abraham)?
Ibn Majah records what happened to the Quranic verse on stoning adulterers after the Prophet's death. What does the hadith say occurred?
questions.a: Early caliphs deliberately removed it as inconsistent with Q 24:2
questions.b: A domesticated animal came in and ate the parchment it was written on
questions.c: It was set aside because its chain of narration was found to be weak

questions.q: Aisha narrates in Muslim that a Quranic ruling established how many sucklings create a permanent marriage prohibition. What was the final number?
Q 56:35-37 describes the female companions of Paradise. How does the verse characterise them?
questions.a: The righteous women of this world, elevated and perfected in the afterlife
questions.b: Created entirely anew as devoted virgins of equal age
questions.c: Angels transformed into human form to serve the believers

questions.q: Q 78:33 describes one of the rewards awaiting believers in Paradise. What term does the Saheeh International translation use for these female companions?
In the Mi'raj hadith in Bukhari, who did Muhammad encounter in the first heaven — sitting while souls passed on his right and left, laughing at those on the right and weeping at those on the left?
Q 17:1 describes the night journey. From al-Masjid al-Haram, where did Allah take His servant in a single night?
Muslim records that the Prophet instructed Sahla bint Suhail to breastfeed the adult man Salim. What was the Islamic legal purpose stated in the hadith?
questions.a: To make him a mahram so he could remain in the household without restriction
questions.b: To grant him citizenship rights within the Muslim community
questions.c: To fulfil a religious obligation Salim had been unable to complete in infancy

questions.q: Q 53:19-20 names three pre-Islamic Arab goddesses. What is the name of the third one — 'the other one'?

In Bukhari's Mi'raj account, Moses tells Muhammad to keep returning to Allah to ask for fewer daily prayers — and Allah reduces the number each time. Open both sources. What does this imply about Moses' knowledge versus Allah's initial command of 50 prayers?
questions.a: Moses had greater practical knowledge of human capacity than Allah's initial command reflected
questions.b: Allah was testing Moses to see if he would dare speak up on behalf of the believers
questions.c: Moses was overstepping — the Prophet should have accepted 50 prayers without negotiation
